stimulus: Each evening in Reykjavik, Iceland, a corner of the city’s harbor is bathed in a warm amber glow. The source of the glow is Sumarsnjór, a public installation made of 1,800 recycled fishing floats arranged into a luminous wave. A network of LEDs woven through the floats ______blank the structure from within.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The true subject of the verb is the singular noun "network," not the plural "LEDs" inside the interrupting prepositional phrase "of LEDs woven through the floats." The singular present-tense verb "lights" agrees in number with "network."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural present-tense verb "light" agrees with the lure noun "LEDs" rather than the singular subject "network." Choice C is incorrect because the plural-agreeing "do light" also fails to agree with the singular subject "network" (which would take "does light"). Choice D is incorrect because the plural-agreeing "light up" fails to agree with the singular subject "network" (which would take "lights up").
